text: Jobs in postLLM world, what happened
voiceover: The job market has clearly changed. We are going to look at some of ways AI has altered the landscape and how to create a strategy to target some of the jobs in the market

text: All API programming have been impacted
voiceover: The github demos on kubernetes autoscaling  has 0 code written by me. All of it was generated by chatGPT. This is the terraform code, it worked first time. No looking at error messages, no reading stack overflow posts. No verbose instructions for the llm. This wasnt the case. The LLMs keep on improving and every improvement leads to a dramitic increase in producitvity .SW with APIs, APIs have been turned into RL environments and this part of programming is now automated with verifiers to keep the policy on track.

From Kun Chen, he did a post on AI driven job losses. A year ago job losses were correlated with interest rates and inflation. We didn't have the one shot LLMs we have today. He goes through an example from Amazon where a team of roughly 8 people rewrote sw previously supported by a head count of 1k. They didnt get rid of 1k people but it helps to see the 30k layoffs in context

Kun paints a scenario a Engineering Manager manages a team of 12 ICs pre llm. Then post LLM they only need a single IC running 12 agents in parallel. If ICs are rougnly 10x more productive then they dont neem EMs so they reduced headcount. The blame went to paying for more GPUs. That is only partially true

From Kun Chen, this graph shows the increasing trend of AI driven job displacement. The blue line is the number of jobs lost due to AI, while the red line is the number of jobs created by AI. The gap between the two lines is widening, indicating that more jobs are being lost than created. This trend is expected to continue maybe for a few years. That being said the reverse is true also, The demand for AI Engineers has never been higher. Next we look at an example.
pika just got 80M for headcount expansion and GPUs.

Lets go through each bullet point in the job description and create a starting demo we can build on. Design, develop and maintain scalable GPU infra for training and serving. Start with a scalable web server, swap out the webserver for a LLM running on CPU.
Lets skip this until we get more people onboarded and learning on their own
pod1 is a single webserver with prometheus and grafana attached.

pod2 autoscale go web server pod with prometheus and grafana. The later demos switch the web server for a LLM and run a python benchmark. The Qwen-0.5 model is the smallest model that can run on a macbook CPU.

title: Summary of Results
voiceover: From the MBPP benchmark, 13 coding tasks tested\n9 Correct | 4 Incorrect

code: def zip_tuples(tup1, tup2):\n    return [(a, b) for a, b in zip(tup1, tup2)]
title: Task 81: Incorrect - zip() truncates

code:
from itertools import cycle

def zip_tuples(tup1, tup2):
    if len(tup2) == 0: return []
    return [(a, b) for a, b in zip(tup1, cycle(tup2))]
title: Task 81: Fixed - use itertools.cycle
